lines: section of the American Board of Radiology (ABR) Core and Certifying exams administered through
calendar year 2025.
The guide has undergone a few changes compared to the 2021 version. The primary changes in this
version are the expansion of Artificial Intelligence concepts in Informatics and additional content on
decision support to the regulatory chapter. Details of contrast safety and MR safety have also been
updated, reflecting new published guidelines.
The determination of whether specific NIS topics merit inclusion in the study guide—and on the
exams—is based primarily on two factors. First, material contained in the NIS section should reflect
knowledge that is needed to perform effectively in a modern radiology practice. Second, the public
interest should be served by expecting the examinee to know the material.
Core Elements of Professionalism were deemed to merit inclusion because they reflect basic principles
to which all physicians, including radiologists, should adhere. Core Concepts of Quality and Safety
were included because they reflect underlying principles that drive quality and safety in any complex
environment. Practical Quality and Safety Applications in Healthcare contain quality and safety
strategies as they are applied to healthcare. Practical Safety Applications in Radiology focus on
radiology-specific topics such as MR safety and management of intravenous contrast material.
Reimbursement, Regulatory Compliance, and Legal Considerations in Radiology reflect mechanisms
that external parties use to ensure quality and safety in radiology practice. Informatics and artificial
intelligence represent a growing area where knowledge is integral to the modern practice of Radiology.

lines: section of the American Board of Radiology (ABR) Core and Certifying exams administered during
calendar year 2026.
The guide has undergone a few changes compared to the 2025 version, consisting mainly of updates
reflecting recently published guidelines related to MR safety and contrast administration.
Decisions regarding whether specific NIS topics merit inclusion in the study guide — and on the
exams — are based primarily on two factors. First, material contained in the NIS section should reflect
knowledge that is needed to perform effectively in a modern radiology practice. Second, inclusion of
the material should serve the public interest by ensuring that examinees possess this knowledge.
Core Elements of Professionalism are included because they reflect fundamental principles to which
all physicians, including radiologists, should adhere. Core Concepts of Quality and Safety address
underlying principles that drive quality and safety in complex work environments. Practical Quality
and Safety Applications in Healthcare contain quality and safety strategies as they are applied to
healthcare. Practical Safety Applications in Radiology focus on radiology-specific topics such as MR
safety and management of intravenous contrast material. Reimbursement, Regulatory Compliance,
and Legal Considerations in Radiology reflect mechanisms that external parties use to ensure quality
and safety in radiology practice. Informatics and artificial intelligence represent a growing area where
knowledge is increasingly integral to the modern practice of radiology.

lines: Core and Certifying exams. However, questions on important subspecialty-specific quality and safety
knowledge and skills are also included on the exams that are not included in this guide, especially
those related to nuclear medicine and other procedure-based specialties. Examinees should be
knowledgeable in basic quality and safety practices relevant to all subspecialties regardless of whether
they are included in this study guide. Physics topics, including radiation safety, are on the exam but
not included in the NIS section.

lines: Core and Certifying exams. However, the exams may also include questions on important
subspecialty-specific quality and safety knowledge and skills not addressed in this guide, especially
those related to nuclear medicine and other procedure-based specialties. Additionally, examinees
should be knowledgeable in basic quality and safety practices relevant to all subspecialties regardless
of whether they are included in this study guide. This study guide does not cover medical physics,
including radiation safety, as it is considered a separate topic; examinees should consult the ABR
Physics Study Guide for relevant physics material covered on the respective exams.

[p004] including radiation safety, as it is considered a separate topic; examinees should consult the ABR
[p004] Physics Study Guide for relevant physics material covered on the respective exams.

lines: well as their underlying purpose. Less emphasis is placed on more superficial details, such as the
names of the various regulatory agencies.
This study guide will continue to evolve in future years to reflect continuing changes in the non-
interpretive knowledge and skills needed to practice effectively in a modern radiology practice.
We also draw your attention to the references provided at the end of each chapter. We recommend that
you consult these “deeper” resources, which provide perspective and depth of understanding of the
concepts that are only superficially outlined in this study guide.

left_display_lines: [p001] Noninterpretive
[p001] Skills
[p001] Study Guide
[p001] This study guide is to be used in preparation for all Diagnostic Radiology Core and
[p001] Certifying exams administered through calendar year 2025.
[p002] Table of Contents
[p002] 1.1 ABIM Physician Charter for Medical Professionalism in the New Millennium .............................................. 5
[p002] 2.1.3 2001 Institute of Medicine Report, Crossing the Quality Chasm ...................................................... 10
[p002] 2.2.2 2015 Institute of Medicine Report, Improving Diagnosis in Health Care ........................................... 12
[p002] Chapter 3. Practical Quality and Safety Applications in Healthcare ..................................................................... 20
[p002] Chapter 5. Reimbursement, Regulatory Compliance, and Legal Considerations in Radiology ............................... 53
[p002] 2025 Noninterpretive Skills Study Guide 2
[p003] 2025 Noninterpretive Skills Study Guide 3
[p004] Introduction
[p004] This study guide has been created to assist examinees in preparing for the noninterpretive skills (NIS)
[p004] section of the American Board of Radiology (ABR) Core and Certifying exams administered through
[p004] calendar year 2025.
[p004] The guide has undergone a few changes compared to the 2021 version. The primary changes in this
[p004] version are the expansion of Artificial Intelligence concepts in Informatics and additional content on
[p004] decision support to the regulatory chapter. Details of contrast safety and MR safety have also been
[p004] updated, reflecting new published guidelines.
[p004] The determination of whether specific NIS topics merit inclusion in the study guide—and on the
[p004] exams—is based primarily on two factors. First, material contained in the NIS section should reflect
[p004] knowledge that is needed to perform effectively in a modern radiology practice. Second, the public
[p004] interest should be served by expecting the examinee to know the material.
[p004] Core Elements of Professionalism were deemed to merit inclusion because they reflect basic principles
[p004] to which all physicians, including radiologists, should adhere. Core Concepts of Quality and Safety
[p004] were included because they reflect underlying principles that drive quality and safety in any complex
[p004] environment. Practical Quality and Safety Applications in Healthcare contain quality and safety
[p004] strategies as they are applied to healthcare. Practical Safety Applications in Radiology focus on
[p004] radiology-specific topics such as MR safety and management of intravenous contrast material.
[p004] Reimbursement, Regulatory Compliance, and Legal Considerations in Radiology reflect mechanisms
[p004] that external parties use to ensure quality and safety in radiology practice. Informatics and artificial
[p004] intelligence represent a growing area where knowledge is integral to the modern practice of Radiology.
[p004] The guide covers the majority of general conceptual and practical NIS information contained in the
[p004] Core and Certifying exams. However, questions on important subspecialty-specific quality and safety
[p004] knowledge and skills are also included on the exams that are not included in this guide, especially
[p004] those related to nuclear medicine and other procedure-based specialties. Examinees should be
[p004] knowledgeable in basic quality and safety practices relevant to all subspecialties regardless of whether
[p004] they are included in this study guide. Physics topics, including radiation safety, are on the exam but
[p004] not included in the NIS section.
[p004] Examinees are expected to understand general NIS concepts rather than esoteric details. For example,
[p004] examinees should understand regulatory requirements that are relevant to daily radiology practice, as
[p004] well as their underlying purpose. Less emphasis is placed on more superficial details, such as the
[p004] names of the various regulatory agencies.
[p004] This study guide will continue to evolve in future years to reflect continuing changes in the non-
[p004] interpretive knowledge and skills needed to practice effectively in a modern radiology practice.
[p004] We also draw your attention to the references provided at the end of each chapter. We recommend that
[p004] you consult these “deeper” resources, which provide perspective and depth of understanding of the
[p004] concepts that are only superficially outlined in this study guide.
[p004] 2025 Noninterpretive Skills Study Guide 4

[p001] Study Guide
[p001] This study guide is to be used in preparation for all Diagnostic Radiology Core and
[p001] Certifying exams administered through calendar year 2026.
[p002] Table of Contents
[p002] 1.1 ABIM Physician Charter for Medical Professionalism in the New Millennium .............................................5
[p002] 2.1.3 2001 Institute of Medicine Report, Crossing the Quality Chasm ..................................................... 10
[p002] 2.2.2 2015 Institute of Medicine Report, Improving Diagnosis in Health Care ......................................... 12
[p002] Chapter 3. Practical Quality and Safety Applications in Healthcare .................................................................... 20
[p002] Chapter 5. Reimbursement, Regulatory Compliance, and Legal Considerations in Radiology .............................59
[p002] 2026 Noninterpretive Skills Study Guide 2
[p003] 2026 Noninterpretive Skills Study Guide 3
[p004] Introduction
[p004] This study guide has been created to assist examinees in preparing for the noninterpretive skills (NIS)
[p004] section of the American Board of Radiology (ABR) Core and Certifying exams administered during
[p004] calendar year 2026.
[p004] The guide has undergone a few changes compared to the 2025 version, consisting mainly of updates
[p004] reflecting recently published guidelines related to MR safety and contrast administration.
[p004] Decisions regarding whether specific NIS topics merit inclusion in the study guide — and on the
[p004] exams — are based primarily on two factors. First, material contained in the NIS section should reflect
[p004] knowledge that is needed to perform effectively in a modern radiology practice. Second, inclusion of
[p004] the material should serve the public interest by ensuring that examinees possess this knowledge.
[p004] Core Elements of Professionalism are included because they reflect fundamental principles to which
[p004] all physicians, including radiologists, should adhere. Core Concepts of Quality and Safety address
[p004] underlying principles that drive quality and safety in complex work environments. Practical Quality
[p004] and Safety Applications in Healthcare contain quality and safety strategies as they are applied to
[p004] healthcare. Practical Safety Applications in Radiology focus on radiology-specific topics such as MR
[p004] safety and management of intravenous contrast material. Reimbursement, Regulatory Compliance,
[p004] and Legal Considerations in Radiology reflect mechanisms that external parties use to ensure quality
[p004] and safety in radiology practice. Informatics and artificial intelligence represent a growing area where
[p004] knowledge is increasingly integral to the modern practice of radiology.
[p004] The guide covers the majority of general conceptual and practical NIS information contained in the
[p004] Core and Certifying exams. However, the exams may also include questions on important
[p004] subspecialty-specific quality and safety knowledge and skills not addressed in this guide, especially
[p004] those related to nuclear medicine and other procedure-based specialties. Additionally, examinees
[p004] should be knowledgeable in basic quality and safety practices relevant to all subspecialties regardless
[p004] of whether they are included in this study guide. This study guide does not cover medical physics,
[p004] including radiation safety, as it is considered a separate topic; examinees should consult the ABR
[p004] Physics Study Guide for relevant physics material covered on the respective exams.
[p004] Examinees are expected to understand general NIS concepts rather than esoteric details. For example,
[p004] examinees should understand regulatory requirements that are relevant to daily radiology practice, as
[p004] well as their underlying purpose. Less emphasis is placed on superficial details, such as the names of
[p004] the various regulatory agencies.
[p004] This study guide will continue to evolve in future years to reflect continuing changes in the
[p004] noninterpretive knowledge and skills needed to practice effectively in a modern radiology practice.
[p004] We also draw your attention to the references provided at the end of each chapter. Examinees are
[p004] encouraged to consult these deeper resources, which provide additional perspective and depth beyond
[p004] the high-level overview presented in this study guide.
[p004] 2026 Noninterpretive Skills Study Guide 4
